hero.body: I build fast, scalable web applications where clean architecture meets intentional design. From high-performance Next.js interfaces to robust backends with Encore.ts, I create digital products built to scale and engineered to convert.

philosophy.body: I don't just build layouts — I build systems. Every project is approached with a product mindset, prioritizing structure, scalability, and real-world business impact. Whether it's a brokerage platform or a custom SEO-driven solution, the goal is simple: efficiency, reliability, and execution.
philosophy.p1_body: Every feature is weighed against business outcomes — not just technical elegance.
philosophy.p2_body: Systems designed to handle 10x growth without rewrites or firefighting.
philosophy.p3_body: Shipping working software is the only metric that matters in the real world.

work.p1_desc: Full-stack trading and investment platform with real-time data feeds, portfolio management, and secure transaction processing at scale.
work.p2_desc: High-performance microservices infrastructure serving 50M+ requests/day with sub-2ms p99 latency, built entirely in TypeScript.
work.p3_desc: ML-driven content optimization platform that analyzes competitive gaps and auto-generates SEO strategies, boosting organic traffic by 3x.
work.p4_desc: End-to-end client delivery platform combining CMS, analytics, and deployment automation for rapid business site launches.
work.p5_desc: Zero-runtime design system with 60+ accessible components, Storybook docs, and automated visual regression testing.

1.overview: A full-stack brokerage platform built to handle the demands of real-time trading — live order books, portfolio analytics, and secure transaction processing under concurrent load. The platform serves active traders who expect millisecond-level responsiveness and zero data loss.
1.problem: The core challenge was reconciling WebSocket live feeds with persistent PostgreSQL state while maintaining strict transactional guarantees. Every price tick needed to propagate to clients instantly without flooding the database, and every trade had to clear atomically with no race conditions.
1.features: Real-time order book with live bid/ask updates|Portfolio analytics dashboard with P&L tracking|Secure multi-step transaction processing|Role-based access control for traders and admins|Full audit log for compliance and dispute resolution
1.stack_next: App Router with RSC for near-zero JS on static views
1.stack_encore: Type-safe RPC layer replacing REST boilerplate
1.stack_postgres: Primary relational store with row-level security
1.stack_redis: Sub-millisecond pub/sub for live price ticks
1.stack_websocket: Persistent duplex channel for real-time order book

2.overview: A high-performance microservices infrastructure built entirely in TypeScript using Encore.ts — replacing a fragile REST monolith with a typed service graph capable of handling 50M+ requests per day at sub-2ms p99 latency.
2.problem: The previous system suffered from network contract drift: 12+ services communicating over undocumented REST APIs, causing silent failures and unpredictable latency spikes under load. The team needed a way to enforce type-safe contracts across service boundaries without introducing language heterogeneity.
2.features: Type-safe gRPC contracts across all service boundaries|Kubernetes auto-scaling with custom HPA metrics|Redis-backed distributed rate limiting|Distributed tracing with full request lineage|Zero-downtime rolling deployments via Encore CI/CD
2.stack_encore: Typed RPC framework generating gRPC stubs automatically
2.stack_typescript: Single language across the entire service mesh
2.stack_kubernetes: Container orchestration with custom autoscaling policies
2.stack_redis: Shared cache and rate-limiter across service boundaries

3.overview: An ML-driven content optimization platform that analyzes SERP data, identifies competitor keyword gaps, and auto-generates SEO strategy briefs — eliminating the manual research bottleneck that was costing content teams weeks of work per campaign.
3.problem: SEO teams were spending 80% of their time on research and only 20% on execution. Keyword gap analysis required manually cross-referencing multiple tools, and content briefs were written from scratch each time — creating an unsustainable bottleneck as client volume scaled.
3.features: SERP competitor gap analysis with AI scoring|Automated content brief generation via OpenAI|Content quality scoring against target keywords|Sitemap auto-update on brief publication|Vercel edge caching for sub-100ms dashboard loads
3.stack_next: Frontend dashboard with server-side data fetching
3.stack_python: Data pipeline and ML inference service
3.stack_openai: GPT-4 for content brief generation and scoring
3.stack_postgres: Persistent store for keyword data and campaign history
3.stack_vercel: Edge deployment for global low-latency access

4.overview: An end-to-end client delivery platform that unified CMS, analytics, and deployment automation into a single dashboard — replacing 6 disconnected tools and slashing project delivery time for a growing web agency.
4.problem: Client hand-offs required manual data migration between a CMS, an analytics provider, a staging host, and a deployment pipeline. Every new site launch meant the same 4-hour setup ritual. The agency needed a single system that could go from brief to live in under a day.
4.features: Sanity CMS with custom schema per client vertical|One-click deployment pipeline to Vercel|Embedded real-time analytics dashboard|White-label client portal for content updates|Framer Motion page transitions for polished delivery
4.stack_next: App Router for fast SSG/ISR across all client sites
4.stack_sanity: Headless CMS with structured content and live preview
4.stack_tailwind: Utility-first styling for rapid design iteration
4.stack_framer: Micro-animations and page transitions for client wow-factor

5.overview: A zero-runtime design system with 60+ accessible components built for internal use across multiple Next.js projects — then open-sourced after it proved its value by eliminating design drift across 8 separate codebases.
5.problem: Without a shared component contract, the same UI patterns were being reimplemented differently across every project. Button variants, form states, and modal behaviors diverged over time, making cross-project consistency impossible and code reviews slow.
5.features: 60+ accessible components built on Radix UI primitives|Full Storybook documentation with live examples|Automated visual regression tests on every PR|Dark and light theme tokens via CSS custom properties|Tree-shakeable bundle — zero unused CSS shipped
5.stack_react: Component architecture with compound component patterns
5.stack_typescript: Strict typing for all component props and variants
5.stack_radix: Accessible primitives as the behavioral foundation
5.stack_storybook: Interactive docs and visual regression test runner

building.headline: Always Shipping
building.agency_body: I am currently scaling a web agency dedicated to delivering end-to-end digital solutions. We combine design, engineering, and SEO to help businesses grow through high-quality web products.
building.rd_body: Deep-diving into Encore.ts and TypeScript to push the boundaries of backend performance, ensuring the systems I build today are ready for the traffic of tomorrow.

about.headline: The Human Behind the Terminal
about.body: While backend architecture is my foundation, I have a deep appreciation for clean layouts and intuitive user interfaces. I consider myself a lifelong learner, constantly exploring new design trends and engineering patterns to bridge the gap between complex logic and stunning digital experiences.

contact.body: Currently taking on select projects through my agency and exploring innovative collaborations.

built_messages: Built with Next.js and coffee that gets cold before the task is done
Built with Next.js and coffee made faster than the deployment
Built with Next.js and coffee that runs out while writing the first function
Built with Next.js and coffee that disappears with every refresh
Built with Next.js and coffee that runs faster than the bugs
Built with Next.js and coffee that finishes before I understand the problem
Built with Next.js and coffee that's gone while I'm still setting up
